title: Employment discrimination based on sexual orientation or gender identity
subtitle: Includes discrimination in hiring, promotion, termination and harassment at work. Only legal status considered, not enforcement.
caption:
Employment discrimination based on sexual orientation or gender identity, 2025
Includes discrimination in hiring, promotion, termination and harassment at work. Only legal status considered, not enforcement.

The world map displays the legal status of employment protections against discrimination based on sexual orientation or gender identity across countries in 2025. The visualization uses a color-coded legend to categorize nations into seven distinct groups: those protecting both sexual orientation and gender identity (dark blue), gender identity only (green), sexual orientation only (light green), those where protections vary by region (yellow), ambiguous status (orange), no protections (red), and areas with no data (grey). The data shows that the most extensive legal protections, covering both sexual orientation and gender identity, are concentrated in North America, including the United States and Canada, as well as across South America in countries like Brazil and Argentina. Australia and New Zealand also fall into this category, alongside a significant cluster of nations in Western and Northern Europe, such as the United Kingdom, France, and Germany. In contrast, the red color indicating no legal protections dominates the map geographically, covering the entirety of the Russian Federation, China, and the vast majority of the African continent, including Nigeria and Egypt. This lack of protection extends through the Middle East and into parts of Southeast Asia. A smaller number of countries, including India and Pakistan, appear in green, signifying protections for gender identity only. Yellow areas, where laws vary by region, include Mexico and Japan. The chart notes that these figures represent legal status only and do not account for enforcement, while also excluding international aviation from the scope. The visual distribution presents a clear divide where comprehensive legal frameworks exist in the Americas and Western Europe, while large contiguous landmasses in Asia and Africa show an absence of statutory employment discrimination protections.
